title: varronia lineata
intro: Varronia lineata, also known as the 'Bitterbush', is a plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and bitter leaves. Traditionally, Varronia lineata has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Varronia lineata is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
